title: lysiana spathulata subsp. spathulata
intro: Lysiana spathulata subsp. spathulata, also known as the Tasmanian blueberry ash, is a species of flowering plant in the family Cunoniaceae, native to Tasmania. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Lysiana spathulata subsp. spathulata include its potential to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to support immune function. Herbal preparations of Lysiana spathulata subsp. spathulata include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
